questions.text: Which keyword is used in JavaScript to declare a variable that cannot be reassigned?
options: var
let
const
static

questions.text: In React, what is the primary purpose of the useState hook?
options: To manage side effects
To add state to functional components
To navigate between routes
To fetch data from an API

questions.text: Which of the following is a common way to define a type for an object in TypeScript?
options: interface
function
class
import

questions.text: Which HTTP method is typically used to create a new resource on a server?
options: GET
POST
PUT
DELETE

questions.text: What does the 'async' keyword do when placed before a function in JavaScript?
options: It makes the function run in a separate thread
It prevents the function from being called twice
It ensures the function always returns a Promise
It stops the execution of the script until the function finishes

questions.text: In Node.js, what is the name of the built-in module used to handle file system operations?
options: path
http
os
fs

questions.text: Which of the following is a standard format for exchanging data between a client and a server?
options: JSON
HTML
CSS
SQL

questions.text: In React, what is the purpose of the 'key' prop when rendering lists?
options: To style individual list items
To identify which items have changed, been added, or removed
To link the list to a specific database record
To prevent the list from re-rendering

questions.text: Which command is commonly used to install new packages in a Node.js project using npm?
options: npm start
npm update
npm install
npm run

questions.text: What does TypeScript add to JavaScript?
options: New runtime behaviors
Static type checking
A new way to handle asynchronous code
A built-in web server

questions.text: In a React component, what is the primary consequence of using a non-memoized function as a prop passed to a child component wrapped in React.memo?
options: The child component will fail to render correctly.
The child component will re-render on every parent re-render despite no prop changes.
The application will throw a 'useMemo' error in the console.
The child component will be hoisted to the top-level scope.

questions.text: A Node.js application is experiencing high CPU usage during a large JSON parsing operation. How should this be handled to prevent blocking the Event Loop?
options: Wrap the parsing logic in an async/await block.
Use a Web Worker to handle the parsing.
Move the parsing logic to a Worker Thread or a child process.
Increase the memory limit using the --max-old-space-size flag.

questions.text: You are implementing a TypeScript interface for a user object where the 'iddleName' property is optional but the 'lastName' is required. Which syntax is correct?
options: interface User { middleName?: string; lastName: string; }
interface User { middleName: string | undefined; lastName: string; }
interface User { middleName: string | null; lastName: string; }
interface User { middleName?: string | null; lastName: string; }

questions.text: In an Express.js application, what happens if an error is thrown inside an asynchronous middleware function without being passed to 'next()'?
options: The error is automatically caught by the global error handler.
The server will automatically restart.
The request will hang until a timeout occurs.
The process will crash immediately.

questions.text: When using React's useEffect hook, what is the result of providing an empty dependency array []?
options: The effect runs on every single re-render.
The effect runs only once after the initial mount.
The effect runs only when the component unmounts.
The effect runs only when the component's state changes.

questions.text: You need to optimize a database query in a Node.js application that retrieves 10,000 records. Which approach is most efficient for memory management?
options: Fetch all records at once and filter them in JavaScript.
Use a database cursor or stream the results in chunks.
Increase the heap size of the Node.js process.
Use Promise.all() to fetch all records in parallel.

questions.text: In TypeScript, what is the difference between 'unknown' and 'any'?
options: There is no difference; they are aliases.
'unknown' is more restrictive because it requires type checking before operations.
'any' is more restrictive because it prevents all property access.
'unknown' can only be used with interfaces.

questions.text: A React application is suffering from 'Prop Drilling'. Which of the following is the most appropriate solution for global state management?
options: Using Redux or the Context API.
Passing props through intermediate components.
Using local state in the root component.
Moving all logic to a Node.js backend.

questions.text: In a RESTful API design, which HTTP method should be used to update a resource by replacing the entire existing resource with new data?
options: PATCH
POST
PUT
UPDATE

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of using a 'Key' prop when rendering a list of elements in React?
options: To uniquely identify elements for CSS styling purposes.
To help React identify which items have changed, been added, or been removed.
To prevent the application from crashing during re-renders.
To assign a unique ID to the component's state.

questions.text: In a high-concurrency Node.js environment, how would you prevent 'Event Loop Lag' when processing a CPU-intensive cryptographic operation?
options: Use Worker Threads to offload the computation to a separate thread.
Wrap the synchronous function in a Promise.resolve().then() call.
Increase the maxOldSpaceSize memory limit of the V8 engine.
Use setImmediate() to break the operation into smaller chunks.

questions.text: When designing a React application with deep component trees, how do you minimize unnecessary re-renders caused by context provider updates?
options: Wrap the provider's value in useMemo and split the context into smaller, specialized providers.
Use React.memo on every single component within the provider's tree.
Use a global state management library like Redux instead of Context API.
Pass the context value through props instead of using the useContext hook.

questions.text: In TypeScript, when implementing a generic repository pattern, how do you ensure that a method can only be called with types that satisfy a specific interface constraint?
options: By using the 'extends' keyword in the generic type declaration.
By using the 'implements' keyword on the class itself.
By using the 'atisfies' operator on the method return type.
By using a type guard function within the method body.

questions.text: Consider a distributed system using Node.js. How would you handle the 'Thundering Herd' problem when a high-traffic cache key expires?
options: By implementing a probabilistic early recomputation (PER) or using a mutex/lock on the cache miss.
By increasing the TTL (Time To Live) of the cache key dynamically.
By using a distributed pub/sub mechanism to notify all instances.
By implementing a circuit breaker pattern around the database connection.

questions.text: While debugging a memory leak in a long-running Node.js process, you notice the heap usage increases steadily. What is the most likely cause if the heap dump shows many objects tied to a 'etTimeout'?
options: Closures within the setTimeout callback capturing large variables in the outer scope.
The Event Loop being blocked by synchronous I/O operations.
The V8 garbage collector being unable to perform a full sweep.
Too many active file descriptors being opened by the process.

questions.text: When optimizing a React application's performance, what is the primary risk of over-using 'useCallback' for every function definition?
options: Increased memory overhead and CPU cost of dependency array comparisons.
It causes the component to skip the reconciliation phase entirely.
It prevents the garbage collector from cleaning up stale closures.
It triggers an infinite loop if used inside a useEffect hook.

questions.text: In a TypeScript codebase, what is the architectural implication of using 'any' instead of 'unknown' when handling API responses?
options: 'any' disables type checking entirely, while 'unknown' forces type narrowing before access.
'any' is more performant at runtime than 'unknown'.
'unknown' is a subtype of 'any', making it more restrictive.
There is no architectural difference; it is purely a matter of linting preference.

questions.text: You are experiencing a race condition in a React component where an API call returns after the component has unmounted. What is the most modern and robust way to handle this?
options: Using an AbortController to signal the fetch request to cancel.
Using a boolean flag (isMounted) inside a useEffect cleanup function.
Wrapping the state update in a try-catch block to ignore errors.
Using a global loading state to prevent further user interaction.

questions.text: When scaling a Node.js API, why might using a 'Cluster' module be less effective than using a container orchestrator like Kubernetes?
options: Cluster only scales vertically on a single machine, whereas Kubernetes scales horizontally across nodes.
Cluster is limited to a single CPU core regardless of the machine size.
The Cluster module is deprecated in recent versions of Node.js.
Kubernetes handles asynchronous I/O much more efficiently than Node.js.

questions.text: In a SQL database used by a Node.js application, how does 'Transaction Isolation Level' affect performance and data integrity?
options: Higher isolation levels increase data integrity but decrease concurrency and performance.
Lower isolation levels increase performance but guarantee no race conditions.
Isolation levels have no impact on performance, only on data visibility.
The 'Read Uncommitted' level is the most performant and safest for all transactions.
